{{Infobox character
| name = Alin
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Eliadel, Rising Sun, leader or spokesperson for the Myrians
| abilities = Traveler powers, shroud his hands in misty white-gold light, manifest a translucent sword of light, access the gold light of Elysia, manifest a golden sword of pure, translucent light from Elysia, wrench a bolt of light from Elysia, conjure a ball of golden light, conjures a golden hound, blast streams of gold light from his palm, uses Elysia and his Territory to reinforce a green shield ever more effectively than others can through sheer force of will alone (Note: text says 'uses Elysia and his Territory to reinforce a green shield')
| affiliations = Myrians, a party from Myria
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Alin Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Alin is the son of [[Torin]] and was born in [[Myria village]], located within the realm governed by [[Overlord Malachi]]. Though he initially expressed uncertainty regarding his status as a [[Traveler]], it is noted that he was born as one of the most powerful [[Territories]]. He possesses the ability to manifest misty white-gold or deadly golden light from his hands and can summon creatures from his [[Territory]], such as a winged, [[steel]]-eyed entity. Alin is identified as an [[Incarnation]], and there are mentions of him potentially being the [[Incarnation of Elysia]]. Throughout his journey, he has interacted with individuals from [[Enosh]] and has expressed a desire to protect those close to him, specifically his sister and [[Leah]]. His experiences include navigating the massive walls of [[Elysia]] and encountering a [[Valinhall]] [[Traveler]].}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Born in [[Myria village]] to [[Torin]], Alin is a vibrant storyteller known for his radiant confidence and gleaming gold hair. As an [[Elysian Traveler]], he possesses the ability to access the light of [[Elysia]], allowing him to manifest translucent swords, conjure golden hounds, and blast streams of light from his palms. While acting as a spokesperson for the [[Myrians]], he took his heroic responsibilities to heart and ordered [[Simon]] to evacuate the villagers so that he could enter the city to rescue [[Leah]]. During his struggles, Alin faced being separated from his body by [[Rhalia]] and used his powers to reinforce a green shield, eventually returning to [[Enosh]] covered in burns, scrapes, and bruises.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a storyteller in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2">''House of Blades'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is tall, strong, vibrant, and has dark gold hair.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] possesses an [[aura]] of radiant confidence.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has gold hair.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c3">''House of Blades'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has a dirty cloth bandage on his head and is bleeding from his scalp.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] radiates sunlight and has hair that gleams like polished gold.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]]'s power caused a flash of golden light that made the storm in front of [[Simon]] vanish.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is the son of [[Torin]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] possesses [[Traveler]] powers.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can shroud his hands in misty white-[[gold light]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can manifest a translucent [[sword]] of light.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] was born in [[Myria village]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is an [[Elysian Traveler]] who has been in [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has had a crush on [[Leah]] since she arrived in the village.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is [[Torin]]'s son and a person capable of taking care of people at the capital.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c5">''House of Blades'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a person to whom things might be left if [[Kai]] leaves.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c6">''House of Blades'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] is waiting for [[Alin]] to do something.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c7">''House of Blades'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is someone [[Simon]] hopes can save the people of [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c10">''House of Blades'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has taken the hero business too closely to heart.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c13">''House of Blades'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] has a second cousin who is a young man from [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a person [[Simon]]'s village members compare him to.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is considering traveling through [[Naraka]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is referred to by the title [[Eliadel]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is described as a Rising Sun.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a poor liar.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can access the [[gold light]] of [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can manifest a golden [[sword]] of pure, translucent light from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can wrench a bolt of light from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is wearing fine clothes that have been through a forest of thorns and a house fire.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a leader or spokesperson for the [[Myrians]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is part of a party from [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can conjure a ball of golden light.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] intends to go into the city to rescue [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] orders [[Simon]] to take the people out of the city to keep them safe.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] believes he and [[Simon]] are capable of things others are not.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a [[Traveler]] who conjures a golden hound.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c18">''House of Blades'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]]'s suit was torn and he had a patch of black ash over his heart.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20">''House of Blades'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] radiates angelic light and has golden hair.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can blast streams of [[gold light]] from his palm.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] is a boy who had previously claimed he would fight [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]]'s mind was separated from his body by [[Rhalia]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] uses [[Elysia]] and his [[Territory]] to reinforce a green shield.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can summon a broad-bladed [[sword]] of light.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21">''House of Blades'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can open one Gate each day.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] can only Travel from one end of [[Bel Calem]] to the other.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] wants [[Alin]] to get to safety.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c22">''House of Blades'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] returned to [[Enosh]] covered in burns, scrapes, and bruises.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After defeating the [[Overlord Malachi]] and taking his mask, Alin escaped with [[Leah]] while tending to recent injuries. Known as [[Eliadel]], or 'The Rising Sun,' this [[Incarnation]] serves the [[Grandmasters]] and [[Enosh]], utilizing the golden power of [[Elysia]] to manifest various magical abilities such as green shields, golden swords, and rose-colored healing light. During a battle observing [[King]] [[Zakareth]] and the [[Enosh Travelers]], Alin used intersecting green plates and golden light to combat the fray before ultimately killing the [[King]] with a [[sword]] of golden light. Following a journey through [[Myria]], Alin demonstrated immense power by utilizing red light for superhuman strength and orange light to soar on wings. Throughout these events, Alin remained focused on achieving results while navigating complex loyalties and the loss of sisters to a powerful creature.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] took the mask from [[Overlord Malachi]]'s shoulders as his head rolled across the floor.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] needs [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] was recently injured and required bandages.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] was accompanied by [[Leah]] during his escape from [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] defeated an [[Overlord]] named [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] possesses the golden power of [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] has huge [[Elysian bears]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is giving a speech from a palace balcony.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] wears gold armor.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] sits at the head of the table under a gold banner featuring a winged [[sword]], point-down, against a rising sun.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] questioned the purpose of aiding the escaped [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] lent [[Simon]] a filiar.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] arrived after [[Simon]] spoke with [[Malachi]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] has sisters who were butchered by an insanely powerful creature.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is known by the name [[Eliadel]], which means 'The Rising Sun' in an ancient language.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is a character who accompanied [[Grandmaster Naraka]] and [[Gilad]] to [[Simon]]'s room.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] possesses a globe of [[Elysian]] light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is focused on getting results.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can gather bright golden force between his palms.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can reach out to [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon a green shield from [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can open a Gate and retreat to his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon a golden [[sword]] made of interlocking planes of light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is traveling through the city of [[Elysia]] with [[Rhalia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is able to open doors.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] craved the light and wanted recognition from [[Leah]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] works for [[Enosh]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is a character who was involved in an operation to destroy the [[Hanging Tree]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is a person who works for [[Grandmasters]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is present during the negotiation with [[Talos]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] sat and said little while processing the situation.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon rose light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] wore armor that was heavy and hot.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] was on the verge of stepping into Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] shook off [[Grandmaster Naraka]] and jogged through the trees.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon a globe of gold.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can throw up two walls of intersecting green plates.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is observing the battle between [[King]] [[Zakareth]] and the [[Enosh Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] used a translucent green light to catch the [[King]]'s spear.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] fired a blast of golden light from his off hand.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] used a green wall to recover during combat.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon beings from the [[Gold District]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is curious about the price [[King]] [[Zakareth]] paid for his strength.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] summons a [[sword]] of golden light to kill [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] guessed the staircase was usually hidden underneath a secret panel in the tile.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] stepped out of the [[Naraka Gate]] into [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] returned from [[Myria]] feeling weary.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] has a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon golden power from his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] possesses a golden [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] used a cushion of orange light to catch [[Tamara]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon rose-pink light to heal injuries.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can manifest a green shield of interlocking plates ten layers thick.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]]'s body was filled with power by blood-red light swirling around his limbs.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] used red light to gain superhuman strength and struck the [[Incarnation]] in the jaw.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can soar on wings of orange light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]]'s body can burst into flames.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon an instinctive shield of green.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon orange light to stop in midair.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can sense things with silver light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can summon an orb of liquid blue light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can call a meteor of golden power from the sky.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] can lift into the air on sunset wings.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin's eyes]] are made of shifting slices of color.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] is an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Alin, the son of [[Torin]] and an [[Incarnation]] from [[Myria]], began reshaping the world around him by reconstructing the city of [[Enosh]] and sculpting blossoms from pink light. As he claimed to be [[Elysia]], his radiant presence allowed him to transform objects, lock down the city at night, and eventually kill [[Grandmaster Tartarus]]. Though he wielded various powers like [[Blue]], [[Green]], [[Orange]], and [[Violet Light]], his immense strength made him appear as a walking natural disaster compared to other [[Incarnations]]. After fighting side-by-side with [[Simon]] and giving up his humanity, Alin eventually ended up on the [[Elysian]] fields with rainbow-colored eyes.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is believed by [[Simon]] to be an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c4">''City of Light'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is the [[Elysian Incarnation]] and son of [[Torin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c5">''City of Light'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] needs to figure something out regarding [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is a person [[Simon]] thinks about.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is an [[Incarnation]] who has begun to reshape the world around him.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] practically locks down the city at night.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] possesses a radiant presence that can transform objects into new forms.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] claims to be [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is a character capable of sculpting flower blossoms out of bright pink light.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] meditated on his own virtue.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] flew back over the city.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] has promised to personally put a stop to [[Alin]] if he lets the [[Elysian Incarnation]] take too much control.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c9">''City of Light'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is an [[Incarnation of Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] has been reconstructing the city of [[Enosh]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] killed [[Grandmaster Tartarus]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is described as a walking natural disaster compared to the rest of the [[Incarnations]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] walked through the doors.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] experiences the suffering of his body in a distant way.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can summon [[Blue Light]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can use [[Green Light]] to form a shell of six-sided plates.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Breaking [[Alin]]'s [[Green]] armor causes him a piercing headache and painful mental feedback that can disable him for a few seconds.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can use [[Violet Light]] to attempt to banish objects.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can use [[Orange Light]] to create a bubble that lifts objects.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is levitating to his feet.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can use the [[Incarnation of Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] possesses [[Rose Light]] which can close wounds almost instantly.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] was from [[Myria]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13">''City of Light'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] had rainbow-colored eyes that shifted and changed when he was alive.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]]'s body can rise like a puppet pulled by invisible strings.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] has rainbow eyes that can freeze mid-swirl.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin's eyes]] can change to a blazing gold color.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] can fill a street with a river of white-hot light.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin's eyes]] changed from gold to luminescent violet.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] holds the [[Lightning Spear]] in his right hand.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is an [[Incarnation]] who has been described as powerful and all-but-immortal.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is no longer considered a [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] wears a mask.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c16">''City of Light'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] has been feeling a sense of doom since his fight with [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is capable of carrying on intelligible conversations.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] might be able to send bears to [[Leah]]'s group.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] may be able to call up the City's whole army.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] felt fury pounding in his heart.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]]'s skin is a shade paler than it had ever been.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] and [[Simon]] working together have the best chance of finishing off an [[Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] provides information about the house in the [[Violet District]] that can hide people from [[Lirial scouts]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] hovers over the city glowing with red and orange light.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] fought side-by-side with [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] gave up his humanity.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Shai]] appears to be using one of [[Alin]]'s powers.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] is on the [[Elysian]] fields.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28">''City of Light'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin's eyes]] look like rainbows.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c28" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Then we must act now. If we Travel through Naraka, surely we can make it.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Brothers and sisters!” Alin exclaimed, throwing out his arms.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“I’m ordering you to take them out of the city, Simon.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|“Simon, you and I can do things that nobody else here can do.”<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“Without him, Leah and I would never have escaped with our lives.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Myrians]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
